question.text: What are the risk factors a Regulated Entity must consider while conducting a Business Risk Assessment under the IFSCA Guidelines?

answer.text: Type of customers and their activities, and countries or geographic areas of engagement
Products, services, delivery channels, and the complexity and volume of transactions
Development of new products and use of new or developing technologies

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The Business Risk Assessment must consider: (i) type of customers and activities; (ii) countries or geographic areas; (iii) products, services, delivery channels and activity profiles; (iv) complexity and volume of transactions; (v) development of new products and business practices; and (vi) use of new or developing technologies.
**The Trap:** All six factors must be considered 'to the extent applicable and relevant.' The exam may list any combination — recognize that the correct answer encompasses all of them.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Business risk assessment is covered in both IFSCA-01 and Series XXIV. In IFSCA-01, the international dimension (countries/geographic areas) carries extra weight due to IFSC's global client base.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** New products and technologies must be risk-assessed PRIOR to launch. This is a forward-looking requirement — don't wait until after launch to assess ML/TF risks.
